summary: Gate-detector honesty: never label a paid post 'entitled' from the rendered-vs-wordcount ratio alone; entitlement now requires an authenticated fetch plus the per-user is_viewed field.
reason: Substack returns a substantial preview for paid posts anonymously, so a word-count ratio ≥0.8 can look like a full body even when unauthenticated. internal/substack/render.go previously risked reporting an anonymous read as 'entitled'; it now reports tier 'full' for a high-ratio anonymous read and only reports 'entitled' when the fetch was authenticated and the post carries is_viewed. This keeps the full-vs-preview / entitlement signal honest — a review-driven correction. Verified live: free→full; paid anon→preview (817/1469=0.56); paid authed→entitled (1463≈1469).
validated_outcome: Live gate verified across free, paid-anonymous, and paid-authenticated reads; hermetic render tests pin the tier labels.
